content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 4, 2007

Abigail M. Cruz
Staff Counsel
Department of Rehabilitation
2000 Evergreen Street, Ste 200
Sacramento, CA 95815-3832

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-06-223

Dear Ms. Cruz:

We are in receipt of your November 28, 2006 letter requesting informal assistance under the provisions of the Political Reform Act ("The Act.")1. The Fair Political Practices Commission may only provide formal written advice to someone whose duties are in question under the Act or to their authorized representative. (Government Code Section 83114(b).). The duty to comply with the Act would rest upon the hypothetical individual who would be seeking to contract with the Department of Rehabilitation upon leaving their position there. Regulation 18329(b)(2) (copy enclosed) specifies:

"(2) Requests for formal written advice will not be acted upon unless the following requirements are met:

(A) The name, title or position, and mailing address of the person whose duties are in question are provided. In addition, if the request is submitted by an authorized representative, it shall contain a specific statement that such authorization has been made."

Additionally, your inquiry is hypothetical, as you stated during our phone conversation, which under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) is a circumstance where the FPPC may decline to provide written advice. If you should have a specific question about a specific official, please contact us again for advice with that individual's consent. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 and a revolving door fact sheet for your review.

1 Government Code sections 81000–91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Sukhi Brar
Counsel, Legal Division
